when_to_use: cinematic noir/dark jazz, muted trumpet over dark piano, extreme rubato drag, ppp-pp dynamics, scene-form arc, 35-65 BPM

defaults.feel: ultra-dragged/rubato - only the brushed-snare swirl stays near the grid, everything else floats behind it

signature_moves: the detective's walk (minor line cliche): Am(maj7) -> Am7 -> Am6 -> Fmaj7(#11) -> E7(b9,b13)
the rainy alleyway (modal interchange): Cm9 -> Fm9 -> Abmaj7(#11) -> G7alt
cluster friction voicing: semitone pairs placed mid-chord (see noir-jazz-genre.md SS4)
cadence_options: no hard cadence needed - a scene can simply decay into silence rather than resolve (see arrangement_defaults)
avoid: plain major triads or bright major 7ths - swap for maj7#11 or m(maj7) so the mood doesn't read as cheerful (see noir-jazz-genre.md SS4, common pitfalls)
dense, fast harmonic rhythm - this genre holds a chord for a full 2+ bars minimum, letting it decay (see noir-jazz-genre.md SS2)

hook_archetypes.name: mournful-3-note-call
hook_archetypes.rhythm: a short 3-note muted-trumpet phrase over 1-2 bars, then silence
hook_archetypes.contour: a slow bend/glissando into a blue note (b5 or b3) from below
hook_archetypes.first_appears: Scene 2 (The Shadow), bars 1-2, answered by a single low piano chord in bars 3-4

hook_archetypes.name: decaying-chord-signature
hook_archetypes.rhythm: one struck chord left to ring and fully decay across an entire phrase
hook_archetypes.contour: Am(maj7) or a cluster voicing, no restrike until it has fully decayed
hook_archetypes.first_appears: Scene 1 (The Alleyway), the first piano entrance

alleyway-intro.note_lengths: near silence - a single bass pluck every 2 bars, one chord left to decay over 4 bars
alleyway-intro.contour: n/a - establishing space before any melody enters

shadow-theme.note_lengths: short mournful phrases, long sustained notes, generous silence between them
shadow-theme.contour: blue-note bends into the target pitch, per the muted-trumpet technique (noir-jazz-genre.md SS7)
shadow-theme.placement: extremely dragged - +30 to +60ms behind the brushed-snare swirl (see groove_profile)

confession-interlude.note_lengths: sparse duet phrasing between sax and piano
confession-interlude.contour: sub-tone tenor sax in a low register, breath-forward rather than pitch-forward
confession-interlude.placement: same extreme drag as shadow-theme, trumpet silent

resolution-outro.note_lengths: the theme restates, then thins to a single bass pluck and one final decaying chord
resolution-outro.contour: molto ritardando - the tempo itself slows into the fade
resolution-outro.placement: drag increases further as the tempo slows, ending in total silence

drum_skeleton.low_energy: constant soft brushed-snare swirl only, no kick, ride occasionally fading in on the '1'
drum_skeleton.mid_energy: swirl continues, a near-inaudible soft-felt kick added on 1 and the 'and' of 3, ride fades on 1 and 3
drum_skeleton.high_energy: not applicable in the usual sense - noir jazz's ceiling is Scene 2/3's full trio, not a louder drum pattern; density comes from adding voices (sax duet), never from a harder-hitting drum layer

arrangement_defaults.layout_rules: Scene 1 (Alleyway/Intro): bass alone, then one piano chord left to decay; Scene 2 (Shadow/Main Theme): brushed swirl enters softly, muted trumpet states the theme over the line-cliche progression; Scene 3 (Confession/Interlude): trumpet drops out, sub-tone tenor sax duets with piano, brief modulation; Scene 4 (Fog/Ambient Break): drums and bass stop entirely, only piano reverb tail and a single delayed trumpet note remain; Scene 5 (Resolution/Outro): full ensemble restates the theme, tempo slows naturally, ends on a single bass pluck and a decaying chord

anti_boredom_rules: every chord must be left to decay fully before the next is struck - no dense harmonic rhythm, minimum ~2 bars per chord (see noir-jazz-genre.md SS2)
cut roughly half the melody notes a first draft would include - space is a requirement, not an option (see common pitfalls)
at least one full scene (Scene 4/fog break) must drop drums and bass entirely, leaving only piano reverb tail and a single held/delayed note
dynamics stay ppp-pp throughout - no sudden swell or forte moment anywhere in the arrangement
the mournful-3-note-call hook must recur, varied by register or answering instrument, never copy-pasted identically
if an ambient/foley texture is used at all, it must sit -18 to -24 dB under the music and be band-passed 400 Hz-5 kHz (see noir-jazz-genre.md SS3) - and it is always acceptable to omit it entirely
